docs.title: Quasar Engine (PD) Programmer's Docoment
docs.text: This document is targeted towards the development of Quasar Engine and its plugins. For User's Decument (UD) visit Quasar Engine (UD) User's Decoment.

docs.title: Platforms
docs.text: Quasar Engine is designed to be cross compatable between Windows, Mac and Linux. A single Codebase is managed for all three of these platforms. On Windows and Linux native implementation of Vulkan is used, while on Mac the Metal translation layer MoltenVK is used.

docs.title: Environment Setup
docs.text:
Windows
  • Visual Studio - Visual Studio C++ devkit for Windows platform.
  • CMake - CMake is a coss platform C++ build tool.
  • Vulkan - Graphics and compute API Vulkan SDK.

Clone GitHub repo and with CMake run build. Make sure to clone the repo recursively.

docs.title: Project Layout
docs.text:
QuasarEngine
|-Quasar                        # Engine dynamic library
|   |-src
|   |   |-Core
|   |   |-Event
|   |   |-Math
|   |   |-Platform
|   |   |-Renderer
|   |-Include
|   |-Vendor
|-Editor                        # User executable application
|   |-src
|-Assets
|   |-shaders                   # Assets to be copied to bin

docs.title: Application
docs.text:
Quasar class QS_API 

Quasar/src/Core/Application.h

Author

Rounak Paul

Dated: 19th March 2024

Abstract

Quasar::Application is the singleton object responsible for the following:

  • Init Quasar Engine and maintain states of various components, eg. Window, Renderer etc.
  • Hosts the main application loop. This is where the main event poling is done.
  • Shutdown the Quasar Engine on exit. Issue commands to release memory, physical decice and safely shutdown the engine.

docs.title: get_instance
docs.text:
public static

static Application& get_instance()
InputOutputInfo

None

Application instance reference.

Get reference to the singleton Quasar Application instance

Tip

Use the macro QS_APPLICATION to get the refence to the singleton Application instance.

#define QS_APPLICATION Application::get_instance()

docs.title: get_main_window
docs.text:
public

Window& get_main_window()
InputOutputInfo

None

Reference to the Main window used by singleton Application.

Get reference to the main window used by the Engine.

Tip

Use the macro QS_MAIN_WINDOW to get the refence to the window used by main Application.

#define QS_MAIN_WINDOW QS_APPLICATION.get_main_window()

docs.title: get_app_state
docs.text:
public

app_state& get_app_state()
InputOutputInfo

None

Reference to the Application state managed by the Engine.

This state is created and passed to the engine at the time of Application creation.

Tip

Use the macro QS_APP_STATE to get the refence to the Application state.

#define QS_APP_STATE QS_APPLICATION.get_app_state()

docs.title: run
docs.text:
public

void run();
InputOutputInfo

None

Void

Hosts the main application loop.

docs.title: Defines
docs.text:
typedef define

Quasar/src/Core/Defines.h
Author

Rounak Paul

Dated: 19th March 2024

docs.title: Types
docs.text:
Important

Use the following defined types in Quasar Engine to avoid varying size of types from compiler to compiler. The following have been static asserted to have the expected size. This size is very important to avoid unknown behavours in varipus modules like Renderer or Event system.

Unsigned int types

Quasar symbol typedef u8 unsigned char u16 unsigned short u32 unsigned int u64 unsigned long long

Signed int types

Quasar symbol typedef i8 signed char i16 signed short i32 signed int i64 signed long long

Floating point types

Quasar symbol typedef f32 float f64 double

Boolean types

Quasar symbol typedef b32 int b8 bool

docs.title: Defines
docs.text:
#define TRUE true

#define FALSE false

#define INVALID_ID_U64 18446744073709551615UL

#define INVALID_ID 4294967295U

#define INVALID_ID_U16 65535U

#define INVALID_ID_U8 255U

Windows

// API export
#define QS_API __declspec(dllexport)

// API import
#define QS_API __declspec(dllimport)

Mac & Linux

// API export
#define QS_API __attribute__((visibility("default")))

// API import
#define QS_API

// Clamp
#define QS_CLAMP(value, min, max) ((value <= min) ? min : (value >= max) ? max : value)

// Min
#define QS_MIN(x, y) (x < y ? x : y)

// Max
#define QS_MAX(x, y) (x > y ? x : y)

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
Quasar class QS_API 

Quasar/src/Core/Input.h

Author

Rounak Paul

Dated: 19th March 2024

Abstract

Quasar::Input is the interface for input event handlers. Quasar Engine is using GLFW events to listen to input events.

docs.title: init
docs.text:
public static

static b8 init()
InputOutputInfo

None

b8: TRUE if input event handlers were set to GLFW Key and Mouse callbacks successfully

Set glfwSetKeyCallback() and glfwSetMouseButtonCallback()

docs.title: is_key_pressed
docs.text:
public static

static void is_key_pressed(GLFWwindow* window, int key, int scancode, int action, int mods)
InputOutputInfo
  • GLFWwindow* window - Pointer to the Main GLFW window object
  • int key - keycode of the keyboard key press event
  • int scancode - N/A
  • int action - N/A
  • int mods - N/A

void

sets context.data.u16[0] = key and Executes event QS_EVENT.Execute(action ? EVENT_CODE_KEY_PRESSED : EVENT_CODE_KEY_RELEASED, 0, context) with the context.

docs.title: is_mousebtn_pressed
docs.text:
public static

static void is_mousebtn_pressed(GLFWwindow* window, int button, int action, int mods);
InputOutputInfo
  • GLFWwindow* window - Pointer to the Main GLFW window object
  • int button - mouse button of the mouse key press event
  • int action - N/A
  • int mods - N/A

void

sets context.data.u16[0] = button and Executes event QS_EVENT.Execute(action ? EVENT_CODE_BUTTON_PRESSED : EVENT_CODE_BUTTON_RELEASED, 0, context) with the context.

glm::vec2 of (x, y) positions of mouse relative to the Window

Get MouseX and MouseY relative to the top left corner of the Main window

docs.text:
Quasar 

Quasar/src/Core/Keycodes.h

Author

Rounak Paul

Dated: 19th March 2024

Abstract

Quasar::Keycodes to be used in Keyboard events in Quasar Engine.

docs.title: MouseCode
docs.text:
// From glfw3.h

QS_MBTN_1                = 1,
QS_MBTN_2                = 2,
QS_MBTN_3                = 3,
QS_MBTN_4                = 4,

QS_MBTN_RIGHT            = QS_MBTN_1,
QS_MBTN_MIDDLE           = QS_MBTN_2

docs.title: Log
docs.text:
Quasar class QS_API 

Quasar/src/Core/Log.h

Author

Rounak Paul

Dated: 19th March 2024

Abstract

Quasar::Log is the logging pipeline for Quasar Engine and Any application using this Engine. Logging as been enabled via some defines to easily be able to put logs to this pipeline.

docs.title: Quasar Core Logger
docs.text:
Info

Core logger is to be used in the devlopment of Quasar Engine Dynamic Library.

usage examples

  • QS_CORE_FATAL 

    QS_CORE_FATAL("fatal event! code %d", fatal_code)

  • QS_CORE_ERROR 

    QS_CORE_ERROR("error event! code %s", __line__)

  • QS_CORE_WARN 

    QS_CORE_WARN("warning event! value %d not expected", value)

  • QS_CORE_INFO 

    QS_CORE_INFO("Vulkan supported GPUs found %d", device_count)

  • QS_CORE_DEBUG 

    QS_CORE_DEBUG("Created main window!")

  • QS_CORE_TRACE 

    QS_CORE_TRACE("FPS: %f", 1/dt)

docs.title: Quasar Application Logger
docs.text:
Info

App logger is to be used in the devlopment of editor or any games created with Quasar Engine.

usage examples

  • QS_APP_FATAL 

    QS_APP_FATAL("fatal event! code %d", fatal_code)

  • QS_APP_ERROR 

    QS_APP_ERROR("error event! code %s", __line__)

  • QS_APP_WARN 

    QS_APP_WARN("warning event! value %d not expected", value)

  • QS_APP_INFO 

    QS_APP_INFO("Vulkan supported GPUs found %d", device_count)

  • QS_APP_DEBUG 

    QS_APP_DEBUG("Created main window!")

  • QS_APP_TRACE 

    QS_APP_TRACE("FPS: %f", 1/dt)
